summary: Session 3 of 4 (backlog-remediation recipe + harvester dogfood, non-final, ships D6; ai_router code + docs, NO release): shipped the one-time over-budget remediation strategy. NEW ai_router/guidance_triage.py: a routed bulk-triage helper that classifies every heading-delimited block of an over-budget lessons file as keep-active|archive|promote|merge|drop, projects the post-remediation active-tier size against the D5 ceiling, and writes an operator-reviewed PROPOSAL without ever editing the target file. Design: permissive block extraction over a mixed ##/### file (the real day-one shape -- byte-exact partition via original-text offset slicing); the classification step is routed (analysis), the deterministic surface (extraction/projection/JSON-parse/batching) is pure and unit-tested with an injected fake route_fn; truncation-aware batching (detect_truncation + halving); raw routed output persisted UTF-8 before any console display; ASCII-folded stdout (cp1252) while the report file keeps fidelity; reference-graph guard (whole-token id match + survivors-only) flags archiving a referenced lesson. NEW docs/guidance-backlog-remediation.md: portable engine-agnostic recipe (measure -> routed triage proposal -> operator review -> supersession-merge dedup -> archive-bankruptcy as a heavily-gated opt-in -> seed last-used-set for survivors -> re-measure), archive != delete throughout, no unattended deletion, no vector-DB. DOGFOOD (read-only, $0.6313 opus single-batch, complexity-hint 90 to force tier-3 and dodge the tier-2 gemini-pro thinking_budget=-1 JSON trap): classified all 69 harvester blocks 0-unclassified -> 44 keep / 20 archive / 5 drop / 0 merge / 0 promote, projecting ~38695 -> ~21815 tokens (~44% reduction) but STILL 2.18x over the 10k ceiling -- the honest headline finding that validates why the recipe carries the merge-dedup + archive-bankruptcy + topic-split escalations. Harvester docs/planning unchanged (verified git status; only pre-existing unrelated set-012 files dirty). s3-harvester-dogfood.md records the proof; s3-harvester-triage-{raw.txt,report.txt} are the artifacts. SUITES: Python 1300 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed (was 1270; +30 new triage tests). TS untouched by S3 (S3 = Python + docs only); its 2 failing tests (configEditor-foundation, notificationsSection) are the long-standing tracked Set-026 baseline. VERIFICATION: gpt-5-4 cross-provider, 3 rounds. R1 ISSUES_FOUND (2 Major: split/join dropped boundary-newlines in size accounting; malformed merge over-counted savings -- + 3 Minor: ref-guard substring/removed-source, cp1252 test vacuous, doc 'persist before parse' overclaim) all fixed; R2 confirmed 4/5 + flagged the residual out-of-range/fold-into-removed merge_target gap; fixed via project_size surviving-target authority; R3 VERIFIED. Structured findings + dispositions in s3-issues.json. Routed spend this session ~$1.12 (dogfood $0.6313 + R1 $0.2750 + R2 $0.1040 + R3 $0.1032 + next-orch analysis $0.0018). No release this session (PyPI keystone + Marketplace template-bundle land in S4).

reason.specifics: S4 is the final session: D7 consumer template-bundle additions (a TypeScript/esbuild change -> Marketplace bump), the cross-repo notice to the over-budget harvester+platform, the canonical engine-agnostic lifecycle doc + docs sweep, and a dual PyPI+Marketplace release through the green-Test gate, plus final close-out. Routed analysis (gemini-pro) recommended claude-code/anthropic/opus at medium effort for a large-context, multi-surface release+docs session, consistent with S1-S3.
